chapter_title.ar: باب التَّشَهُّدِ فِي الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ الْأَوَّلَتَيْنِ وَ الرَّابِعَةِ وَ التَّسْلِيمِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 30 –The performing of the Tashahhud (testimonies) during the first two Rak’at, and the fourth, and the greeting (Salām)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/161)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/161)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ أَدْنَى مَا يُجْزِئُ مِنَ التَّشَهُّدِ فَقَالَ الشَّهَادَتَانِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hajjal, from Sa’albat Bin Maymoun, from Yahya Bin Talha, from Sowrat Bin Kuleyb who said,
‘I asked Abu Ja’farasws about the least of what would suffice from the Tashahhud, so heasws said: ‘The two testimonies’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hajjal, from Tha’labah ibn Maymun from Yahya ibn Talhah from Sawrah ibn Kulayb who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the minimum of what is sufficient for Tashahhud (testimonies of belief). He (the Imam) said, ‘It is the two testimonies of belief.”
